failure_reason: The cited block p011_c0004 reads 'Offset Bus Lanes replace one general travel lane in each direction and maintain curb access'. This block describes what offset bus lanes are but does not state any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. The relationship relies on the document-level context (the page is part of a 125th Street SBS presentation), which constitutes impermissible co-occurrence-in-document reasoning. The cited block text does not itself establish a has_treatment relation to project_125th-st-sbs-2013.

The cited block p006_c0008 reads 'Off-Board Fare Collection'. This block lists a treatment name only. It does not state any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. The belonging to a page titled 'Select Bus Service on the M60' under 'Potential Improvements' relies on document-level context (co-occurrence), which is explicitly not a valid relation per the rules.

The cited block p006_c0011 reads 'Transit Signal Priority reduces time stopped at red lights'. This block describes what TSP does but does not state any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. The relationship relies entirely on the block's placement under 'Select Bus Service on the M60 — Potential Improvements' on page 6, which is document-level context / co-occurrence rather than an explicit statement in the block text itself.

The cited block p006_c0012 reads 'Pedestrian Safety Improvements at key intersections'. This block names a treatment type but does not state any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. The relationship depends on the block's inclusion under the 'Potential Improvements' list for 'Select Bus Service on the M60' — this is co-occurrence in a document, which the rules state is not a valid relation.

The cited blocks p025_c0005 and p025_c0006 describe bus bulbs and their benefits but neither states any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. Block p025_c0005 describes the diagram; block p025_c0006 reads 'Bus bulbs would reduce pedestrian congestion and crossing distances'. Neither block text mentions 125th Street SBS. The relationship relies on the page being within the '+selectbusservice 125th Street' deck and titled 'Proposed Bus Bulbs at Lexington Av', which is document-level co-occurrence.

The cited block p006_c0010 reads 'Updated curb regulations reduce double parking, and make deliveries easier'. This describes what updated curb regulations do but does not state any connection to the 125th Street SBS project. Like the other p006 blocks, it appears under 'Potential Improvements' for 'Select Bus Service on the M60', making the relationship dependent on document-level co-occurrence rather than explicit block-text evidence.
